1. the polynomial equation -2t³ + 15t² + 36t + 100 = 10 is solved by subtracting 10 from both sides to get -2t³ + 15t² + 36t + 90 = 0. then factor out -2 to get -2(t³ - 7.5t² - 18t - 45) = 0. this gives t³ - 7.5t² - 18t - 45 = 0. using the rational root theorem, possible roots are ±1, ±3, ±5, ±9, ±15, ±45. testing t = 5 gives 125 - 187.5 - 90 - 45 = -197.5, not zero. testing t = 6 gives 216 - 243 - 108 - 45 = -174, not zero. testing t = 7 gives 343 - 367.5 - 126 - 45 = -295.5, not zero. testing t = 9 gives 729 - 583.5 - 162 - 45 = 9.5, not zero. testing t = 10 gives 1000 - 750 - 360 - 45 = 125, not zero. no rational root found. therefore, the equation has no simple solution and requires numerical methods to approximate the root.
